deep_dive.title: Actionable Activation Directions for Detecting and Mitigating Emergent Misalignment Across Language Model Families
deep_dive.abstract: Fine-tuning language models on insecure code induces emergent misalignment with poorly understood internal structure. We investigate whether this misalignment corresponds to a causally actionable activation-space direction shared across architectures. Across four instruction-tuned model families (Qwen2.5-1.5B, Gemma-2-2B, Llama-3.2-1B, Ministral-3-3B) finetuned identically, a difference-in-means direction achieves 99.6% separation of aligned and misaligned activations at each model's final layer. Causal steering by subtracting this direction reduces code spillover by 21-51 points, while a secure-code control confirms content specificity. Cross-architecture transfer via ridge regression maps yields large behavioral suppression (up to 46 points) but fails specificity controls as random and orthogonal directions perform comparably. We identify a two-tier specificity structure: within-model directions are causally specific and actionable; cross-model directions are causally real but non-specific. An asymmetric transfer topology emerges, with Gemma and Qwen acting as geometric donors and Llama as a receiver. These findings define the limits of linear cross-architecture correction and recommend within-model probing for auditing.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: uses activation steering to detect and mitigate misalignment from fine-tuning
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用激活引导检测并缓解微调导致的对齐失配
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用激活引导检测并缓解微调导致的对齐失配
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 使用激活引导检测并缓解微调导致的对齐失配
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper shows that fine-tuning on insecure code causes emergent misalignment with a common activation direction. Subtracting this direction via causal steering reduces code spillover by 21-51 points across model families, demonstrating a method for mitigating harmful fine-tuning effects.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 微调不安全代码会导致模型在激活空间中产生共同的对齐失配方向。该论文发现跨模型家族的差异均值方向可以分离对齐与失配激活，并通过因果减除该方向将代码溢出减少21-51分。该方法直接关联激活引导（activation steering）用于缓解有害微调攻击，为生成安全对齐数据提供了可迁移的方向操作手段。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 微调不安全代码会导致模型在激活空间中产生共同的对齐失配方向。该论文发现跨模型家族的差异均值方向可以分离对齐与失配激活，并通过因果减除该方向将代码溢出减少21-51分。该方法直接关联激活引导（activation steering）用于缓解有害微调攻击，为生成安全对齐数据提供了可迁移的方向操作手段。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Activation steering for synthetic safety data generation

quick_skim.title: Data-Centric Benchmarking of Exploit Generation in LLMs: Understanding the Impact of Fine-Tuning
quick_skim.abstract: We study the task of CVE-conditioned exploit generation, where a model drafts proof-of-concept (PoC) exploits given software vulnerability context. We adopt a data-centric approach, constructing a high-quality dataset via multi-stage preprocessing and introducing a scalable evaluation framework with LLM-as-judge and fine-grained rubrics. Under this unified setup, we benchmark 17 large language models across 8 evaluation criteria, providing systematic insights into their zero-shot capabilities. We further show that a compact 8B open-weight model, when fine-tuned on curated data, achieves over 42.5% improvement in exploit quality and rivals some proprietary models when combined with simple test-time rejection strategies. Our results highlight the importance of data quality, structured supervision, and evaluation design for reliable exploit generation, suggesting that these factors can be as critical as model scale in adapting LLMs to cybersecurity tasks.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: studies how fine-tuning affects exploit generation quality, directly relevant to safety degradation from fine-tuning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 研究微调如何影响利用生成质量，直接关系到微调导致的安全退化
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 研究微调如何影响利用生成质量，直接关系到微调导致的安全退化
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 研究微调如何影响利用生成质量，直接关系到微调导致的安全退化
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Benchmarks 17 LLMs on CVE-conditioned exploit generation, showing fine-tuning on curated data improves exploit quality by 42.5%, highlighting fine-tuning risks.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文通过数据为中心的基准测试，研究微调对大语言模型生成安全利用的影响。构建高质量数据集并引入LLM作为评判的评估框架，在17个模型上系统评估。发现一个小型8B模型在微调后利用质量提升42.5%，接近专有模型。这揭示了微调可能加剧安全风险，对理解微调导致的安全退化具有重要意义。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文通过数据为中心的基准测试，研究微调对大语言模型生成安全利用的影响。构建高质量数据集并引入LLM作为评判的评估框架，在17个模型上系统评估。发现一个小型8B模型在微调后利用质量提升42.5%，接近专有模型。这揭示了微调可能加剧安全风险，对理解微调导致的安全退化具有重要意义。

quick_skim.title: Beyond Safe Data: Pretraining-Stage Alignment with Regular Safety Reflection
quick_skim.abstract: To achieve deeper safety alignment for large language models (LLMs), recent efforts have studied how to push safety interventions earlier into the pretraining stage, primarily by filtering unsafe data or rewriting it into safer forms. We argue that pretraining-stage alignment should go beyond making the data safe: LLMs may compose seemingly benign knowledge and capabilities into unsafe behaviors. To this end, we propose Safety Reflection Pretraining, a pretraining-stage alignment method which regularly inserts short safety reflections into pretraining corpora to integrate self-monitoring directly into language modeling, establishing a foundational capability that is subsequently reinforced by compatible post-training. Our experiments with 1.7B models pretrained on FineWeb-Edu show that Safety Reflection Pretraining improves safety classification accuracy and substantially reduces the success rates of inference-stage and finetuning attacks. Complementary to our real-world experiments, we also introduce a fully controlled synthetic environment, MedSafetyWorld, with a clear definition of safety and a reasoning structure under which models can easily generalize unsafe behaviors from safe data. Ablations in MedSafetyWorld further demonstrate a clear advantage of Safety Reflection Pretraining in preventing models from acting on unsafe behaviors generalized from safe data, compared with data filtering and rewriting. Taken together, our findings suggest that pretraining alignment should not only make the training data safe, but also shape the behaviors that models are likely to acquire from safe data.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: proposes inserting safety reflections into pretraining corpora for alignment preservation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出在预训练语料中插入安全反思以保持对齐
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出在预训练语料中插入安全反思以保持对齐
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出在预训练语料中插入安全反思以保持对齐
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper proposes Safety Reflection Pretraining, which inserts short safety reflections into pretraining data to establish self-monitoring. Although at pretraining stage, the data curation idea of regularly integrating safety content is transferable to fine-tuning data scheduling for alignment preservation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出安全反思预训练方法，在预训练语料中规律插入简短安全反思，使语言模型内建自我监控能力。尽管阶段不同，但其数据调度（定期插入安全内容）的思想可直接迁移到微调阶段：通过混合安全对齐数据来缓解安全退化。实验证明该方法在1.7B模型上提升了后续对齐效果。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文提出安全反思预训练方法，在预训练语料中规律插入简短安全反思，使语言模型内建自我监控能力。尽管阶段不同，但其数据调度（定期插入安全内容）的思想可直接迁移到微调阶段：通过混合安全对齐数据来缓解安全退化。实验证明该方法在1.7B模型上提升了后续对齐效果。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: research on data curation and scheduling for preserving model alignment during fine tuning
